text:
COMPREHENSIVE SOMATIC TUMOR GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT
Integrated with Germline BRCA1/2 Reflex Testing

========================================================
SPECIMEN INFORMATION
========================================================
Patient Name: Margaret Osei
Date of Birth: [on file]
Accession Number: ONC-2024-TGP-08814
Ordering Physician: Gynecologic Oncology Service
Report Finalized By: [Molecular Pathology Attending, MD, PhD]
Specimen Type: Formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tissue block
Specimen Source: Omental biopsy (peritoneal implant)
Procedure: Diagnostic laparoscopy / omental biopsy
Date of Procedure: [see procedure record]
Tumor Nuclei Estimate: 75% (reviewed by pathologist prior to DNA extraction)
DNA Quality: Adequate for sequencing (DIN 4.1; 28 ng/uL input)
DNA Yield: Sufficient for panel completion
Test Performed: Comprehensive tumor genomic profiling (CGP), 523-gene targeted panel with copy number analysis, fusion detection, tumor mutational burden (TMB), microsatellite instability (MSI), and homologous recombination deficiency (HRD) scoring

========================================================
METHODOLOGY
========================================================
Platform: Hybrid-capture next-generation sequencing (Illumina NovaSeq 6000)
Panel Coverage: 523 cancer-relevant genes; full exonic sequence plus selected introns for fusion detection; coverage of hotspot regions
Mean Coverage Depth: 742x (tumor); 218x (matched normal leukocyte DNA for germline subtraction)
Sensitivity: Single-nucleotide variants (SNVs) and small insertions/deletions (indels): variant allele frequency (VAF) threshold >= 5% (analytical sensitivity ~99% at VAF >= 10%); copy number alterations: >=4 copies (amplification) or <=1 copy (loss); gene fusions: read-pair and split-read analysis across selected fusion-relevant genes
HRD Scoring: Genomic instability score derived from allele-specific copy number analysis (LOH, telomeric allelic imbalance [TAI], large-scale state transitions [LST]); HRD positive defined as score >= 33
TMB: Calculated from somatic non-synonymous mutations per megabase (Mb) across the coding regions of the panel; TMB-high defined as >= 10 mut/Mb per institutional threshold
MSI: Fragment analysis and NGS-based assessment of 114 microsatellite loci; MSI-high defined as >= 30% of loci unstable

Note: This assay detects somatic alterations in tumor DNA. Matched normal leukocyte DNA was used for germline subtraction to improve specificity for somatic calls. Separately, germline BRCA1/2 testing was performed on a clinical germline testing platform (see Germline Testing section below).

========================================================
GENOMIC FINDINGS SUMMARY
========================================================

--- SOMATIC ALTERATIONS DETECTED ---

Gene        | Alteration                     | Classification   | VAF     | Exon/Codon  | Notes
------------|--------------------------------|------------------|---------|-------------|----------------------------------------------
TP53        | p.R248W (c.742C>T)             | Pathogenic       | 68%     | Exon 7      | Hotspot GOF mutation; diffuse strong p53 IHC consistent
BRCA1       | Allelic loss (LOH)             | Pathogenic       | --      | Multi-exon  | Loss of heterozygosity at BRCA1 locus; biallelic inactivation in context of germline PV (see below)
CCNE1       | Copy number gain (3 copies)    | VUS / Low-level  | --      | Chr 19q12   | Low-level gain; not meeting amplification threshold (< 6 copies); uncertain clinical significance
MYC         | Copy number gain (3 copies)    | VUS / Low-level  | --      | Chr 8q24.21 | Low-level gain; not meeting amplification threshold; uncertain clinical significance

--- SOMATIC ALTERATIONS NOT DETECTED (Selected Genes) ---

Gene        | Status
------------|-------------------------------------------------------------
BRCA2       | No somatic pathogenic alteration detected
PIK3CA      | Wild-type (no hotspot or pathogenic variant detected)
KRAS        | Wild-type
NRAS        | Wild-type
CDK12       | Wild-type; no biallelic loss detected
PTEN        | Wild-type
RB1         | Wild-type; no biallelic loss detected
NF1         | Wild-type
ARID1A      | Wild-type
BRAF        | Wild-type
MEK1/MAP2K1 | Wild-type
FGFR1/2/3   | Wild-type; no amplification
ERBB2       | Wild-type; no amplification
MET         | Wild-type; no amplification
AKT1/2/3    | Wild-type
FOXL2       | Wild-type
NTRK1/2/3   | No fusions detected
RET         | No fusions detected
ALK         | No fusions detected
ROS1        | No fusions detected
NRG1        | No fusions detected

========================================================
HRD / GENOMIC INSTABILITY PROFILING
========================================================
HRD Score: 54
HRD Status: POSITIVE (threshold >= 33)
Components:
  - LOH (loss of heterozygosity segments >= 15 Mb): 16 segments (elevated)
  - TAI (telomeric allelic imbalance): 11 (elevated)
  - LST (large-scale state transitions): 27 (elevated)
Interpretation: This tumor exhibits a genomic instability signature consistent with homologous recombination deficiency. In the clinical context of a germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant with somatic LOH at the BRCA1 locus (biallelic inactivation), the elevated HRD score reflects functional BRCA1 loss and defective HR-mediated DNA repair. HRD positivity is associated with sensitivity to platinum-based chemotherapy and PARP inhibitors.

========================================================
TUMOR MUTATIONAL BURDEN (TMB)
========================================================
TMB: 5 mutations/Mb
Interpretation: LOW (< 10 mut/Mb). Not TMB-high. This finding is typical for high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma, which is characterized by a low somatic mutation rate driven primarily by chromosomal instability rather than mutational accumulation. TMB-high designation is not met; pembrolizumab per TMB-high indication does not apply in this context.

========================================================
MICROSATELLITE INSTABILITY (MSI) STATUS
========================================================
MSI Status: MICROSATELLITE STABLE (MSS)
114 microsatellite loci assessed; 0% unstable loci detected.
Interpretation: MSS. No evidence of mismatch repair (MMR) deficiency by this assay. This finding is consistent with the expected biology of high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma (MMR deficiency is uncommon, < 2% of HGSOC). Immunotherapy based on MSI-H/dMMR designation is not applicable.

========================================================
PD-L1 EXPRESSION
========================================================
PD-L1 Expression (Combined Positive Score, CPS): 2
Interpretation: LOW PD-L1 expression (CPS < 10). Assessed by IHC on the same FFPE omental biopsy specimen using 22C3 pharmDx antibody. PD-L1 low status is noted; however, this is a companion diagnostic assessment for informational purposes in this tumor context.

========================================================
GERMLINE BRCA1/2 REFLEX TESTING
(Reported from Separate Clinical Germline Testing Platform)
========================================================
Germline Variant Identified:
  Gene:            BRCA1
  cDNA Change:     c.5266dupC
  Protein Change:  p.Gln1756ProfsTer74
  Classification:  PATHOGENIC
  ClinVar:         Pathogenic (multiple submitters; RCV000048640)
  Variant Type:    Frameshift (insertion/duplication); predicted to cause premature stop codon and nonsense-mediated decay of BRCA1 transcript
  Inheritance:     Autosomal dominant; heterozygous germline carrier
  Penetrance:      High-penetrance; lifetime risk of breast cancer approximately 69-72% (PRSS estimates); lifetime risk of ovarian cancer approximately 44-46% (PRSS estimates)
  Zygosity in Tumor: Biallelic loss (germline PV + somatic LOH at BRCA1 locus confirmed on tumor NGS)

BRCA2 Germline Status: No pathogenic variant detected in BRCA2.
Note: BRCA1 c.5266dupC (historically referred to as 5382insC) is among the most prevalent BRCA1 founder mutations and is well-characterized in ClinVar and peer-reviewed literature.

Genetic Counseling: Germline result has been communicated per institutional protocol. Cascade testing recommended for first-degree relatives (siblings, children). Refer to certified genetic counselor.

========================================================
INTERPRETATION
========================================================
This high-grade serous carcinoma of ovarian/peritoneal/tubal origin demonstrates a genomic profile highly characteristic of the HGSOC molecular subtype:

1. TP53 p.R248W (VAF 68%): A gain-of-function hotspot mutation in TP53 is present at high allele frequency, consistent with clonal, biallelic TP53 inactivation (the second hit likely being the wild-type allele loss at 17p, consistent with the elevated HRD score). TP53 mutation is near-universal in HGSOC (~96%) and is consistent with the aberrant (diffuse strong) p53 immunohistochemical pattern observed on the diagnostic biopsy. This variant is not directly actionable therapeutically but confirms the diagnosis.

2. Germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant + somatic LOH (biallelic inactivation): The tumor has undergone biallelic BRCA1 inactivation via the two-hit mechanism: germline frameshift variant (first hit) plus somatic loss of heterozygosity at the BRCA1 locus (second hit). Functional BRCA1 protein is absent in tumor cells, resulting in defective homologous recombination DNA repair. This is the primary driver of HRD positivity and is the most clinically actionable finding in this report.

3. HRD Score 54 (positive): Consistent with an HR-deficient phenotype. This finding, in combination with platinum-based chemotherapy sensitivity, supports eligibility for PARP inhibitor-based maintenance therapy.

4. Low-level CCNE1 and MYC copy number gains: These low-level copy number gains are of uncertain clinical significance. CCNE1 amplification (high-level) is a known mechanism of PARP inhibitor resistance in HGSOC, but low-level gain at 3 copies does not meet the threshold for amplification and its functional significance is unclear at this time. CCNE1 status should be re-evaluated if platinum resistance develops in the future.

5. TMB low, MSS, PD-L1 low: These findings are typical for HGSOC. Standard checkpoint inhibitor indications (TMB-high, MSI-H/dMMR) are not met.

========================================================
ACTIONABILITY SUMMARY BY TIER
========================================================

TIER I — STRONG CLINICAL SIGNIFICANCE (FDA-approved agent for this tumor type / indication)

  Finding: Germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (c.5266dupC) + Somatic BRCA1 LOH (biallelic inactivation) + HRD score positive (54)
  Relevant Drug Class: PARP inhibitors (PARPi)
  FDA-Approved Agents in Ovarian Cancer Context:
    - Olaparib (Lynparza): FDA-approved as maintenance therapy for adult patients with deleterious or suspected deleterious germline or somatic BRCA-mutated advanced epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube, or primary peritoneal cancer who are in complete or partial response to first-line platinum-based chemotherapy (SOLO1 trial basis; approval 2018)
    - Niraparib (Zejula): FDA-approved as maintenance therapy for adult patients with advanced epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube, or primary peritoneal cancer who are in complete or partial response to first-line platinum-based chemotherapy, regardless of BRCA or HRD status (PRIMA/ENGOT-OV26 basis; HRD-positive subgroup showed greatest benefit)
    - Rucaparib (Rubraca): FDA-approved for BRCA-mutated advanced ovarian cancer (recurrent setting after 2+ prior chemotherapy lines)
  Clinical Note: Patient is HRD-positive with germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant. Following completion of first-line platinum-based chemotherapy with response, PARP inhibitor maintenance therapy is strongly indicated. Both olaparib and niraparib are appropriate options per guideline-based care (NCCN Category 1 for germline BRCA1/2 mutation + complete or partial response to first-line platinum).

TIER II — POTENTIAL CLINICAL SIGNIFICANCE (clinical evidence in other tumor types or investigational in this tumor type)

  Finding: Low-level CCNE1 copy number gain (3 copies)
  Note: High-level CCNE1 amplification (>= 6 copies, or focal high-amplitude amplification) has been associated with resistance to PARP inhibitors and platinum-based therapies in HGSOC. The low-level gain observed here (3 copies) does not meet amplification criteria; clinical significance is uncertain. No currently approved therapy specifically targets CCNE1. CDK2 inhibitors targeting the CCNE1/CDK2 axis are under clinical investigation (e.g., investigational CDK2 inhibitors in CCNE1-amplified HGSOC), but this finding does not currently justify enrollment in CCNE1-directed trials given the low copy number.

  Finding: Low-level MYC copy number gain
  Note: Low-level MYC gain is common in HGSOC and of uncertain clinical significance at this level. No approved therapeutic targeting MYC is available. This finding is not actionable at present.

TIER III — UNKNOWN CLINICAL SIGNIFICANCE

  No additional VUS alterations with available clinical evidence identified.

TIER IV — NOT CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT / INFORMATIONAL

  Finding: TP53 p.R248W
  Note: Diagnostic and prognostic significance; not directly targetable with approved therapies. APR-246 (eprenetapopt, a TP53 reactivator) has been studied in ovarian cancer in early phase trials but is not FDA-approved.

  Finding: TMB low (5 mut/Mb), MSS, PD-L1 CPS 2
  Note: Standard checkpoint inhibitor indications not met.

========================================================
QUALITY CONTROL METRICS
========================================================
Percent exons at >= 100x coverage: 97.4%
Uniformity (% bases at >= 0.2x mean coverage): 96.8%
Total reads aligned: 94.1%
On-target reads: 91.3%
Duplication rate: 18.2% (acceptable)
Contamination estimate (FASTQ-screen): < 1%
All QC thresholds met. Results are reportable.

========================================================
LIMITATIONS
========================================================
- This assay detects somatic alterations within targeted gene regions. Non-covered regions (e.g., deep intronic variants, regulatory elements) are not assessed.
- Tumor heterogeneity may result in subclonal alterations below the detection threshold of this assay.
- Copy number analysis is dependent on tumor purity and ploidy estimation; low-level copy number gains may be subject to analytical uncertainty.
- RNA-based fusion detection was not performed on this specimen; a separate RNA panel may be ordered if fusion gene partners are clinically suspected.
- Germline variants in this report are derived from a separate clinical germline testing platform (matched leukocyte DNA); germline findings should be confirmed and interpreted in the context of full clinical history by a board-certified genetic counselor.
- This report reflects findings at the time of analysis and should be interpreted in conjunction with clinical, pathologic, and radiographic data.

========================================================
REPORT ATTESTATION
========================================================
This report was reviewed and approved by the Molecular Pathology attending physician. Results have been communicated to the ordering gynecologic oncology team per institutional protocol. Genetic counseling referral placed for germline BRCA1 result communication and cascade testing coordination.

CAP Accreditation No.: [on file] | CLIA Certificate No.: [on file]
